---
title: "quiz8"
title-block-style: none
format: live-html
engine: knitr
sidebar:
  style: docked
resources:
  - crop_fertilizer_A.csv
  - crop_fertilizer_B.csv
---

{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}
{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_gradethis.qmd >}}

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
  const showQ = params.get("q");

  // Show only the requested section
  if (showQ) {
    document.querySelectorAll("section[id]").forEach(section => {
      if (section.id !== showQ) section.style.display = "none";
    });
  }

  // Hide page title
  document.querySelectorAll(".quarto-title-block")
    .forEach(el => el.style.display = "none");

  // Hide ALL section titles (## ...)
  document.querySelectorAll("section[id] > h2")
    .forEach(h => h.style.display = "none");
});
</script>

## Q1 — One-way ANOVA (manual) {#q1a}
Using the `crop_fertilizer_A.csv` dataset, we test

$H_0 : \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D$

$H_a : \text{At least one population mean yield differs.}$

Compute the F statistic.
```{webr}
# Read the data
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_A.csv")

# Grand mean
grand_mean <- mean(___)

# Group means and sizes
group_means <- tapply(___, ___, ___)
group_sizes <- tapply(___, ___, ___)

# SSR (between-group sum of squares)
SSR <- sum(___ * (___ - ___)^2)

# SST (total sum of squares)
SST <- sum((___ - ___)^2)

# SSE (within-group sum of squares)
SSE <- ___ - ___

# Degrees of freedom
k <- length(___)
N <- nrow(___)

df_reg <- ___
df_err <- ___
df_tot <- ___

# Mean squares
MSR <- ___ / ___
MSE <- ___ / ___

# F statistic
F_stat <- ___ / ___

F_stat
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

# Read the data
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_A.csv")

# Grand mean
grand_mean <- mean(crop$yield)

# Group means and sizes
group_means <- tapply(crop$yield, crop$fertilizer, mean)
group_sizes <- tapply(crop$yield, crop$fertilizer, length)

# SSR (between-group sum of squares)
SSR <- sum(group_sizes * (group_means - grand_mean)^2)

# SST (total sum of squares)
SST <- sum((crop$yield - grand_mean)^2)

# SSE (within-group sum of squares)
SSE <- SST - SSR

# Degrees of freedom
k <- length(group_means)
N <- nrow(crop)

df_reg <- k - 1
df_err <- N - k
df_tot <- N - 1

# Mean squares
MSR <- SSR / df_reg
MSE <- SSE / df_err

# F statistic
F_stat <- MSR / MSE

F_stat
# 72.5
```

## Q1 — One-way ANOVA (manual) {#q1b}
Using the `crop_fertilizer_B.csv` dataset, we test

$H_0 : \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D$

$H_a : \text{At least one population mean yield differs.}$

Compute the F statistic.
```{webr}
# Read the data
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_B.csv")

# Grand mean
grand_mean <- mean(___)

# Group means and sizes
group_means <- tapply(___, ___, ___)
group_sizes <- tapply(___, ___, ___)

# SSR (between-group sum of squares)
SSR <- sum(___ * (___ - ___)^2)

# SST (total sum of squares)
SST <- sum((___ - ___)^2)

# SSE (within-group sum of squares)
SSE <- ___ - ___

# Degrees of freedom
k <- length(___)
N <- nrow(___)

df_reg <- ___
df_err <- ___
df_tot <- ___

# Mean squares
MSR <- ___ / ___
MSE <- ___ / ___

# F statistic
F_stat <- ___ / ___

F_stat
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

# Read the data
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_B.csv")

# Grand mean
grand_mean <- mean(crop$yield)

# Group means and sizes
group_means <- tapply(crop$yield, crop$fertilizer, mean)
group_sizes <- tapply(crop$yield, crop$fertilizer, length)

# SSR (between-group sum of squares)
SSR <- sum(group_sizes * (group_means - grand_mean)^2)

# SST (total sum of squares)
SST <- sum((crop$yield - grand_mean)^2)

# SSE (within-group sum of squares)
SSE <- SST - SSR

# Degrees of freedom
k <- length(group_means)
N <- nrow(crop)

df_reg <- k - 1
df_err <- N - k
df_tot <- N - 1

# Mean squares
MSR <- SSR / df_reg
MSE <- SSE / df_err

# F statistic
F_stat <- MSR / MSE

F_stat
# 48.5671
```

## Q2 — One-way ANOVA (built-in) {#q2a}
Using the `crop_fertilizer_A.csv` dataset, we test

$H_0 : \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D$

$H_a : \text{At least one population mean yield differs.}$


Compute the p-value using the `aov()` function.
```{webr}
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_A.csv")

fit = aov(___ ~ ___, data = ___)

summary(___)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_A.csv")

fit = aov(yield ~ fertilizer, data = crop)

summary(fit)
```

## Q2 — One-way ANOVA (built-in) {#q2b}
Using the `crop_fertilizer_B.csv` dataset, we test

$H_0 : \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D$

$H_a : \text{At least one population mean yield differs.}$


Compute the p-value using the `aov()` function.
```{webr}
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_B.csv")

fit = aov(___ ~ ___, data = ___)

summary(___)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_B.csv")

fit = aov(yield ~ fertilizer, data = crop)

summary(fit)
```

## Q3 — Fisher's LSD {#q3a}

Using the `crop_fertilizer_A.csv` dataset, perform pairwise comparisons using the Fisher's LSD method.

Compute the p-value for the comparison between fertilizers **B** and **D**.

```{webr}
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_A.csv")

pairwise.t.test(___, ___, p.adjust.method = ___)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_A.csv")

pairwise.t.test(crop$yield, crop$fertilizer, p.adjust.method = "none")
# 0.0085  
```

## Q3 — Fisher's LSD {#q3b}

Using the `crop_fertilizer_B.csv` dataset, perform pairwise comparisons using the Fisher's LSD method.

Compute the p-value for the comparison between fertilizers **B** and **D**.

```{webr}
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_B.csv")

pairwise.t.test(___, ___, p.adjust.method = ___)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_B.csv")

pairwise.t.test(crop$yield, crop$fertilizer, p.adjust.method = "none")
# 0.0202
```

## Q4 — Bonferroni Adjustment {#q4a}

Using the `crop_fertilizer_A.csv` dataset, perform pairwise comparisons using the Bonferroni method.

Compute the p-value for the comparison between fertilizers **B** and **D**.

```{webr}
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_A.csv")

pairwise.t.test(___, ___, p.adjust.method = ___)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_A.csv")

pairwise.t.test(crop$yield, crop$fertilizer, p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
# 0.0509
```


## Q4 — Bonferroni Adjustment {#q4b}

Using the `crop_fertilizer_B.csv` dataset, perform pairwise comparisons using the Bonferroni method.

Compute the p-value for the comparison between fertilizers **B** and **D**.

```{webr}
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_B.csv")

pairwise.t.test(___, ___, p.adjust.method = ___)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
crop <- read.csv("crop_fertilizer_B.csv")

pairwise.t.test(crop$yield, crop$fertilizer, p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
# 0.1212
```
